_comment: City of Falls Church Basic and Police Pension Plans
systemName: City of Falls Church Pension Plans
boardComposition: 7 members: 3 appointed by City Council, 1 by City Manager, 1 Police Plan member (elected), 2 Basic Plan members (elected)

planDocument.title: The City of Falls Church Basic Pension Plan

latestAmendment.scope: Section 6.03 — Optional Forms of Annuity reduced from 8 to 5 options

socialSecurityNote: City participates in Social Security (OASDI 6.2% + Medicare 1.45% = 7.65% employer match)

deferredComp457b.note: 457(b) deferred compensation available to all employees

employerMatch401a.note: 401(a) employer match plan separated from 457(b) effective July 1, 2018 so employer funds do not count against employee maximum contribution limits

vrsParticipants.note: Constitutional officers and their full-time employees must join VRS (not the city pension). City makes contributions on their behalf.

basic.planName: City of Falls Church Basic Pension Plan
basic.coverageGroup: All permanent regular employees working 20+ hours/week (except sworn police and constitutional officers)

contributionInterest.note: Contributions accrue interest at 5% per annum compounded annually to Dec 31, 1986; 5.5% per annum from Jan 1, 1987

normalRetirement.ageNote: Normal retirement date is first day of calendar month coinciding with or following attainment of Social Security retirement age (currently 67 for those born 1960+)
normalRetirement.formula: 1.8% x years of credited service x final average compensation (highest consecutive 5 years)

ruleOf90.requirement: Age + years of credited service >= 90, minimum age 50

supplement.note: Additional $200/month paid until member reaches normal retirement date (Social Security retirement age)

reducedEarly.requirement: Age 52 with at least 10 years in plan

nonServiceRelated.formula: 2% of average highest 3-year compensation × years of credited service, max 50% of that compensation
nonServiceRelated.normalRetirementConversion: At NRA, converted to normal retirement formula assuming continued employment at disability-date compensation
nonServiceRelated.medicalReexam: Annual for 5 years, then every 3 years until NRA

serviceRelated.formula: 66⅔% of average highest 3-year compensation, reduced by workers compensation awards

cola.index: CPI-U, U.S. City Average, All Items (Bureau of Labor Statistics)
cola.formula: 50% of CPI-U increase, capped at 4% maximum per year
cola.disabilityRetirees: Pre-12/1/1979 disability retirees: COLA begins July 1, 1994 using October 1993 CPI baseline. Post-12/1/1979: same formula from retirement date.
cola.note: Half of CPI-U increase means if CPI rises 5%, retirees get 2.5%. If CPI rises 10%, retirees get max 4%. No COLA in deflationary years.
cola.source: City of Falls Church Basic Pension Plan, Amended and Restated January 1, 2016 (further amended January 1, 2018 per Resolution 2018-54)

terminationOptions.refund: May withdraw accumulated contributions plus interest
terminationOptions.deferredBenefit: May leave contributions in plan and take deferred benefit at retirement eligibility

defaultForm.description: Life annuity with 120 monthly payments guaranteed. If participant dies before 120 payments, remainder paid to beneficiary.

marriedDefault.description: Joint and 100% survivor annuity with spouse. Actuarial equivalent of normal form.

options.description: Joint and 66⅔% survivor (reduction upon first death)
Joint and 50% survivor (reduction upon first death)
Qualified Joint and 100% survivor annuity with spouse
Joint and 50% survivor (reduction upon retiree death only — spouse predeceasing does not reduce benefit)

postMay2026.keyChange: Reduced from 8 options to 5. Joint 50% survivor redefined: reduction now only upon retiree death (not first death). Removed: 60-month guarantee, 66⅔% survivor, cash refund, installment refund.

activeAfterOct2003.description: 2.0% of Final Average Compensation × years of Credited Service

hiredOnOrAfter2012.description: 1.8% of Final Average Compensation × years of Credited Service

historicFormulas.note: Pre-2003 participants had tiered formulas with SS integration. See Plan sections 5.01(a)(1) and 5.01(a)(2) for details.

earlyReduction.hiredBefore2012: 1/3 of 1% per month before earlier of NRA or Rule of 80
earlyReduction.hiredOnOrAfter2012: 5/8 of 1% per month for first 60 months, then 1/3 of 1% per month, before earlier of NRA or Rule of 90

employer.note: Includes special 2014 one-time $6.3M contribution from water system sale. Annual earnings on that contribution (up to $565K) used in required contribution calculation.

sickLeaveCredit.method: 75% of unused sick leave above threshold

hiredBefore1999_04_26.note: 100% of sick leave above $15,000 value, then 75% applied

hiredOnOrAfter1999_04_26.note: 100% of sick leave above $2,500 value, then 75% applied

priorGovernment.note: Federal, state, or local government with public pension participation

servicePurchase.combined: Military + prior government can stack beyond 4 years each

police.planName: City of Falls Church Police Pension Plan

multiplier.note: 2.8% per year for first 20 years of credited service; 3.0% per year for all years in excess of 20

vesting.note: Fully vested after 5 years of credited service

normalRetirement.rule: Earlier of age 62 OR completion of 25 years in Police Pension Plan
normalRetirement.formula: 2.8% x first 20 years + 3.0% x years over 20, applied to final average compensation

earlyRetirement.note: Unreduced retirement after 20 years of service

normalRetirement.rule: Earlier of age 55 OR (age 50 AND completion of 25 years in Police Pension Plan)
normalRetirement.formula: 2.8% x first 20 years + 3.0% x years over 20, applied to final average compensation

earlyRetirement.note: Normal retirement criteria serve as the early retirement threshold for post-1986 tier

cola.index: CPI-U, U.S. City Average, All Items (Bureau of Labor Statistics)
cola.formula: 50% of CPI-U increase, capped at 4% maximum per year
cola.note: Half of CPI-U increase means if CPI rises 5%, retirees get 2.5%. If CPI rises 10%, retirees get max 4%. No COLA in deflationary years.
cola.authority: City of Falls Church Police Pension Plan Document, as summarized in Section 4, Exhibit F of actuarial valuation
cola.source: City of Falls Church Police Pension Plan Actuarial Valuation as of June 30, 2025 (Segal), Section 4 Exhibit F, page 44

disability.note: Disability retirement available; details in Police Pension Plan document

maxBenefitExamples.basic_30yr: 1.8% x 30 x FAC = 54% of final average compensation
maxBenefitExamples.basic_35yr_rule90: 1.8% x 35 x FAC = 63% of final average compensation
maxBenefitExamples.police_20yr: 2.8% x 20 x FAC = 56% of final average compensation
maxBenefitExamples.police_25yr: (2.8% x 20 + 3.0% x 5) x FAC = 71% of final average compensation
maxBenefitExamples.police_30yr: (2.8% x 20 + 3.0% x 10) x FAC = 86% of final average compensation

researchGaps: COLA formula and rate (Plan Document Section 5.04 exists but full content not extracted)
Specific actuarial reduction factors for reduced early retirement
Police Plan detailed survivor options and death benefits
Post-retirement medical benefits eligibility and contribution details
Current plan assets and funded status
